L. 106–117, title X, § 1036113 Stat. 1595Nov. 30, 1999“(a).—Effective DateThe amendments made by this subtitle [subtitle C (§§ 1031–1036) of title X of , amending this section and sections 7254, 7281, 7296, and 7297 of this title] shall take effect on the date of the enactment of this Act [].Pub. L. 106–117Nov. 30, 1999“(b).—Savings Provision for Incumbent Chief JudgeThe amendments made by this subtitle shall not apply while the individual who is chief judge of the Court [United States Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims] on the date of the enactment of this Act [] continues to serve as chief judge. If that individual, upon termination of service as chief judge, provides notice under , United States Code, of availability for service in a recalled status, the rate of pay applicable to that individual under section 7296(c)(1)(A) of such title while serving in a recalled status shall be at the rate of pay applicable to that individual at the time of retirement, if greater than the rate otherwise applicable under that section.”Nov. 30, 1999section 7257 of title 38

children.heading: .—Composition
children.label: a .—Composition
children.text: The Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims is composed of at least three and not more than seven judges, one of whom shall serve as chief judge in accordance with subsection (d).

children.heading: .—Appointment
children.label: b .—Appointment
children.text: The judges of the Court shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, solely on the grounds of fitness to perform the duties of the office. A person may not be appointed to the Court who is not a member in good standing of the bar of a Federal court or of the highest court of a State. Not more than the number equal to the next whole number greater than one-half of the number of judges of the Court may be members of the same political party.

children.heading: .—Term of Office
children.label: c .—Term of Office
children.text: The term of office of the judges of the Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims shall be 15 years. A judge who is nominated by the President for appointment to an additional term on the Court without a break in service and whose term of office expires while that nomination is pending before the Senate may continue in office for up to 1 year while that nomination is pending.

children.label: A
children.text: have served for one or more years as judges of the Court;

children.label: B
children.text: have at least 3 years remaining in term of office; and

children.label: C
children.text: have not previously served as chief judge.

children.label: A
children.text: In any case in which there is no judge of the Court in regular active service who meets the requirements under paragraph (1), the judge of the Court in regular active service who is senior in commission and meets subparagraph (A) or (B) and subparagraph (C) of paragraph (1) shall act as the chief judge.

children.label: B
children.text: In any case under subparagraph (A) of this paragraph in which there is no judge of the Court in regular active service who meets subparagraph (A) or (B) and subparagraph (C) of paragraph (1), the judge of the Court in regular active service who is senior in commission and meets subparagraph (C) shall act as the chief judge.

Except as provided in paragraph (4), a judge of the Court shall serve as the chief judge under paragraph (1) for a term of five years or until the judge becomes age 70, whichever occurs first. If no other judge is eligible under paragraph (1) to serve as chief judge upon the expiration of that term, that judge shall continue to serve as chief judge until another judge becomes eligible under that paragraph to serve as chief judge.

children.label: i
children.text: the chief judge leaves regular active service as a judge of the Court; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the chief judge notifies the other judges of the Court in writing that such judge desires to be relieved of the duties of chief judge.

children.label: B
children.text: The effective date of a termination of the term under subparagraph (A) shall be the date on which the chief judge leaves regular active service or the date of the notification under subparagraph (A)(ii), as the case may be.

If a chief judge is temporarily unable to perform the duties of chief judge, those duties shall be performed by the judge of the Court in active service who is present, able and qualified to act, and is next in precedence.
Judges who have the same seniority in commission shall be eligible for service as chief judge in accordance with their relative precedence.

children.heading: .—Salary
children.label: e .—Salary
children.text: Each judge of the Court shall receive a salary at the same rate as is received by judges of the United States district courts.

A judge of the Court may be removed from office by the President on grounds of misconduct, neglect of duty, engaging in the practice of law, or violating . A judge of the Court may not be removed from office by the President on any other ground.section 7255(c) of this title
Before a judge may be removed from office under this subsection, the judge shall be provided with a full specification of the reasons for the removal and an opportunity to be heard.

The Court shall prescribe rules, consistent with the provisions of chapter 16 of title 28, establishing procedures for the filing of complaints with respect to the conduct of any judge of the Court and for the investigation and resolution of such complaints. In investigating and taking action with respect to any such complaint, the Court shall have the powers granted to a judicial council under such chapter.

The provisions of sections 354(b) through 360 of title 28, regarding referral or certification to, and petition for review in, the Judicial Conference of the United States and action thereon, shall apply to the exercise by the Court of the powers of a judicial council under paragraph (1) of this subsection. The grounds for removal from office specified in subsection (f)(1) shall provide a basis for a determination pursuant to section 354(b) or 355 of title 28, and certification and transmittal by the Conference shall be made to the President for consideration under subsection (f).

children.label: A
children.text: In conducting hearings pursuant to paragraph (1), the Court may exercise the authority provided under to pay the fees and allowances described in that section.section 1821 of title 28

children.label: B
children.text: The Court shall have the power provided under to award reimbursement for the reasonable expenses described in that section. Reimbursements under this subparagraph shall be made from funds appropriated to the Court.section 361 of title 28
During the period from , through , the authorized number of judges of the Court specified in subsection (a) is increased by two.January 1, 2002August 15, 2005

children.label: i
children.text: only one may be appointed pursuant to a nomination made in 2002; and

children.label: ii
children.text: only one may be appointed pursuant to a nomination made in 2003.

children.label: B
children.text: If a judge is not appointed under this subsection pursuant to a nomination made in 2002, a judge may be appointed under this subsection pursuant to a nomination made in 2004. If a judge is not appointed under this subsection pursuant to a nomination made in 2003, a judge may be appointed under this subsection pursuant to a nomination made in 2004. In either case, such an appointment may be made only pursuant to a nomination made before .October 1, 2004

The term of office and the eligibility for retirement of a judge appointed under this subsection, other than a judge described in paragraph (4), are governed by the provisions of section 1012 of the Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims Amendments of 1999 (title X of ; ; note) if the judge is one of the first two judges appointed to the Court after .Public Law 106–117113 Stat. 159038 U.S.C. 7296November 30, 1999

A judge of the Court as of , who was appointed to the Court before , may accept appointment as a judge of the Court under this subsection notwithstanding that the term of office of the judge on the Court has not yet expired under this section. The term of office of an incumbent judge who receives an appointment as described in the preceding sentence shall be 15 years, which includes any period remaining in the unexpired term of the judge. Any service following an appointment under this subsection shall be treated as though served as part of the original term of office of that judge on the Court.December 27, 2001January 1, 1991

Notwithstanding paragraph (1), an appointment may not be made to the Court if the appointment would result in there being more than seven judges on the Court who were appointed after . For the purposes of this paragraph, a judge serving in recall status under shall be disregarded in counting the number of judges appointed to the Court after such date.January 1, 1997section 7257 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), effective during the period beginning on , and ending on the date of the enactment of the Veterans Benefits Improvement Act of 2024, the authorized number of judges of the Court specified in subsection (a) is increased by two.December 31, 2009

children.label: B
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), effective as of the date of the enactment of the Veterans Benefits Improvement Act of 2024, the authorized number of judges of the Court specified in subsection (a) is increased by three.

Effective as of , an appointment may not be made to the Court if the appointment would result in there being more judges of the Court than the authorized number of judges of the Court specified in subsection (a).January 1, 2028
